scoreboard_footer: * Match ends when one player reaches {0}
achievement_10_plus_target: Achieve target of 10 or more 50 times
achievement_double_nil: Both Partner and Me acheiving NIL together 5 times
help.page1.body: Spades is a 4-player partnership trick-taking card game. Partners sit across from each other. Goal Be the first partnership to reach 500 points. Win at least as many tricks as your combined bid each round.
help.page2.body: Partnership Bidding Each player bids how many tricks they expect to win. Both partners' bids are added together as the team's combined target. • Minimum bid: 0 (Nil bid — see next page) • Maximum bid: 13 • Bids are binding — they cannot change
help.page3.body: Nil Bid Bidding Nil (0) means you aim to win zero tricks this round. Nil Bonus/Penalty Successful Nil: +100 points Failed Nil: -100 points (your partner still plays for their own bid) Blind Nil (bid before seeing cards): +200 / -200
help.page4.body: Made the Bid Won ≥ combined bid: Score = bid × 10 + extra tricks ("bags") Missed the Bid Won < combined bid: Score = −bid × 10 Bags Each overtrick = +1 bag. Every 10 bags = −100 points.
help.page5.body: Playing Tricks You must follow suit if you have a card of the led suit. If you have no cards of the led suit, you may play any card — including a Spade. Leading Spades You cannot lead with a Spade until Spades have been broken (a Spade has been played on a previous trick).
help.page6.body: Spades Are Always Trump A Spade beats any non-Spade card, regardless of rank. If multiple Spades are played, the highest Spade wins. Card Ranks (High to Low) A > K > Q > J > 10 > 9 > 8 > 7 > 6 > 5 > 4 > 3 > 2
help.page7.body: Sandbag Penalty Overtricks (tricks beyond your bid) count as bags. When a team accumulates 10 bags: • −100 points penalty • Bag counter resets to zero Strategy Avoid winning too many extra tricks — bags can cost you the game over time.
